name: Broadcast
summary: Declarative DOM-to-JS bridge for provider-scoped broadcast events. Fills the platform gap between targeted Invoker Commands and global event dispatch.

invokerCommands.gap: No broadcast equivalent — commands are always 1-to-1 via element ID

commandEvent.pattern: CommandEvent is constructable but designed for declarative button triggers, not programmatic broadcast

customEvent.pattern: CustomEvent + dispatchEvent is the current broadcast mechanism
customEvent.gap: Globally-scoped string names — no discrimination, no scoping

naming.rationale: Broadcast clearly communicates 'everyone, do this' intent. Mirrors the event taxonomy from Web Events research. 'dispatch' is too generic (could mean targeted). 'emit' is framework jargon (Node.js EventEmitter, Vue $emit).

dispatchTarget.rationale: Broadcast commands are global by definition. Subtree-scoped broadcast is a separate unsolved problem (DOM events only bubble up, not down). Keep the block simple and single-purpose.

triggerEvent.configurable: broadcast-on attribute for other triggers (e.g., change, submit)
triggerEvent.rationale: Buttons are the primary broadcast trigger. Other elements may need different trigger events.

providerResolution.description: Resolves event class from the injector scope of the host element, constructs an instance, dispatches on document
providerResolution.rationale: The host element's scope determines which event class to use. This allows different parts of the app to broadcast different event classes for the same provider key.

detailSource.description: Optional broadcast-detail attribute for static detail, or broadcast-from attribute referencing a store/context for dynamic detail
detailSource.rationale: Broadcast commands often carry payload (e.g., theme name, locale code). Static JSON covers simple cases; store reference covers reactive cases.
